title: Standardize DataTools Pro Explorer Naming and App Copy
description:
Standardize all naming and copy for the DataTools Pro Explorer Snowflake app:

### Naming Hierarchy
- Product name: "DataTools Pro Explorer"
- Company name: "DataTools Pro" (not GoodmanGroup LLC)
- No references to Streamlit in user-facing copy

### App Copy Updates
- **Short description**: "DataTools Pro Explorer for Snowflake is a simple data exploration tool to aggregate, filter, and sort data quickly while generating SQL code for you."
- **Home page**: Full overview with sections for Target Users, Key Features (Visual Explorer, Schema & Table Exploration, Instant Query Execution, No-Code Analysis, Secure & Native), Installation, Setup & Permissions, and Support
- **Documentation links**: All tutorial URLs should use UTM parameters (`utm_source=snowflake&utm_medium=app&utm_id=dtp_explorer`) and be presented as text links rather than raw URLs

### Installation Instructions
Includes both Snowflake Marketplace and Internal Marketplace installation paths, plus SQL GRANT statements for setting up permissions.
acceptance_criteria: All user-facing copy uses 'DataTools Pro Explorer' as the product name
Company is referenced as 'DataTools Pro', not 'GoodmanGroup LLC'
No references to Streamlit in user-facing copy
Documentation links include UTM parameters and are displayed as text links
App home page includes Overview, Target Users, Key Features, Installation, Setup, and Support sections
Short description matches the specified copy
ux_changes: Complete copy overhaul for the DataTools Pro Explorer app: standardized naming, updated descriptions, structured home page content, and UTM-tagged documentation links.
implementation_notes: All URLs pointing to the tutorial page should use the format: https://datatoolspro.com/tutorials/datatools-pro-explorer/?utm_source=snowflake&utm_medium=app&utm_id=dtp_explorer and should be rendered as hyperlinked text rather than raw URLs.
comment_summary: Reporter clarified that tutorial URLs should always be rendered as hyperlinked text (e.g., 'View our documentation') rather than displaying the raw URL.
